course_description.character: IMPORTANT — this profile does not appear to describe a real, bookable event. Research (2026-07-24) found no adult, public-entry cyclosportive or gran fondo tied to Tour de Romandie branding, in Lausanne or anywhere else (the only branded participant activity is the P'tit Tour de Romandie, a short untimed ride for roughly 15 invited children per stage). The professional Tour de Romandie (UCI WorldTour) is a closed race restricted to Elite Men and Under-23 Men — its official 2026 regulations have no public-registration section, and the official 2026 stage list had no Lausanne start or finish (it opened in Villars-sur-Glâne, with stage towns at Martigny, Rue/Vucherens, Orbe, Broc/Charmey, and Lucens/Leysin). The course description previously published here — 'Jura Foothills,' 'Alpine Approaches,' a 'Lausanne Finale' near the Olympic stadium, and a named 'Romandie Rollercoaster' signature challenge — traces to no official source found in research and has been removed rather than repeated as fact. No organizer record, registration page, results archive, or documented Lausanne course supports the listing; the vitals (100.0 mi / 8,500 ft, converting to 160.9 km / 2,591 m) read like a generic imperial century-ride template rather than a documented Swiss course.

history.origin_story: IDENTITY CORRECTION (2026-07-24): the founded:1947 date and 'amateur sportive inspired by the Tour de Romandie' origin story previously here belong to the PROFESSIONAL Tour de Romandie's own founding (conceived 1946 for the Swiss cycling federation's 50th anniversary, first raced 1947 by ten invited teams of four riders). Research found no evidence that this founding date, or any origin story, applies to a separate amateur cyclosportive — no such currently-bookable event was found. Cleared rather than repeated as fact.

biased_opinion.summary: This profile does not appear to describe a real, current, bookable gran fondo. Web research on 2026-07-24 found no adult, public-entry cyclosportive tied to Tour de Romandie branding — in Lausanne or anywhere in Romandie. The professional Tour de Romandie is a closed UCI WorldTour stage race for Elite Men and Under-23 Men only; its 2026 regulations contain no public-registration section, and the official 2026 stage list had no Lausanne start or finish. No organizer record, registration page, results archive, or documented Lausanne course supports this listing: the vitals on file (100.0 mi / 8,500 ft) convert to tidy round numbers rather than a documented Swiss course, and the citations previously in this profile referenced only the professional race — none supported a public sportive, a Lausanne course, or the 1947 founding date as applied here. A real but now-defunct amateur series called 'Tour de Romandie populaire' did exist (conceived 2011, documented from at least 2013 through 2016; organized by Chassot Concept as separately-named events at venues including Lucens, Aigle-Leysin, Charmey, Neuchâtel, and Verbier) — but no Lausanne edition or course matching this profile's 160.9 km / 2,591 m was found, and it is a different thing from what this profile describes. This is not a defunct race being restored to accuracy; it is an unsupported record that should not be presented to riders as a real event.
weaknesses: No evidence this event currently exists, has ever existed in this form, or is bookable by any rider
The vitals (100.0mi/8,500ft) and previous course description read as generic templated content rather than researched facts about a real Swiss course
The 1947 founding date and origin story belong to the unrelated professional Tour de Romandie, not to any amateur equivalent

The real historical 'Tour de Romandie populaire' series (documented 2013-2016 at venues including Neuchâtel, Aigle-Leysin, and Charmey) is a different thing and should not be conflated with this Lausanne-specific record
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Do not present this to riders as a bookable race. If you want a real, large, well-documented Lausanne-area cyclosportive, ride the Cyclotour du Léman instead (176 km around Lake Geneva, cyclotour.ch) — this profile should be flagged for a human decision on removal from the catalog.

final_verdict.one_liner: Investigated 2026-07-24: no evidence this event exists — not a real, bookable gran fondo.
final_verdict.should_you_race: No — this does not appear to be a real, bookable event. No current Tour de Romandie-branded amateur racing was found; the historical 'Tour de Romandie populaire' series (Neuchâtel/Aigle-Leysin/Charmey and other venues) is documented only through 2016.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a real, large Lausanne-based cyclosportive: Cyclotour du Léman (176 km around Lake Geneva, cyclotour.ch). For Tour de Romandie exposure as a spectator: follow the professional UCI WorldTour stage race itself (tourderomandie.ch) — it has no adult public-entry component.

eligibility.notes: Re-investigated 2026-07-24 (upgraded from a search-engine spot-check to a thorough sourced audit): no evidence found that 'Tour de Romandie Cyclosportive' as described in this profile (160.9km/2591m, Lausanne, 28 April 2026, founded 1947) exists or has existed — high confidence this is an unsupported composite record. The professional Tour de Romandie's 2026 regulations restrict entry to Elite Men/U23 with no public-registration path, and its official 2026 stage list had no Lausanne start or finish. A real but distinct and now-defunct amateur series ('Tour de Romandie populaire,' conceived 2011, documented 2013-2016 at venues including Lucens, Aigle-Leysin, Charmey, Neuchâtel, and Verbier) exists in the historical record but no Lausanne edition or matching course was found. See research-dumps/tour-de-romandie-cyclosportive.md for full research trail. Recommend human review for removal from the catalog.

catalog_flags.status_note: AUTHENTICITY INVESTIGATION (2026-07-24): thorough codex web-search research found no evidence this event exists — not currently, not historically in this form. Identity-corrected this wave per the flandrien-ride.json pattern: unsupported course_description, origin_story, and logistics content removed rather than repeated as fact; biased_opinion now states plainly that this doesn't check out as a real gran fondo. Matching the hedged confidence level used in eligibility.status ('unknown') rather than overclaiming certainty: this is a strong negative-evidence finding — no organizer record, registration page, results archive, or documented course was found after a thorough sourced audit — not a confirmed fabrication with a known origin. FLAG FOR HUMAN: recommend deciding whether to remove this profile from the catalog entirely, since (unlike flandrien-ride or birkebeinerrittet-road) no real underlying event was found to correct toward. The vitals (160.9km/2,591m, Lausanne, 28 April 2026) and the 70/100 fondo_rating score below remain populated only because the schema requires non-null vitals/scores — they are NOT verified facts, see biased_opinion/eligibility for the actual finding; a human should decide whether an unsupported event should carry a displayed score or vitals at all.
